prompt: I have three Haskell records for the same user schema: RawUser with Maybe fields, ValidatedUser with Either errors, and EncodedUser with JSON cells. Design a better typed structure without duplicating the schema.
expected_properties: names one owner of the record shape
selects a carrier-parametric/HKD record
defines carrier semantics for raw/validated/encoded views
places sparse-to-total resolution at a constructor boundary
names typed validation failures and law tests
avoid: three independent records as co-authoritative schemas
dense or encoded view as canonical schema

prompt: We have a closed set of channels. Each channel has a different state type, but every runtime must contain all channels and support typed lookup, traversal, replacement, and diagnostics.
expected_properties: selects a witness-indexed product
defines a closed witness universe
uses a type family for per-index payloads
requires tabulate/index/traverse/replace operations
keeps existential values paired with witnesses

prompt: Design a third-party plugin registry where plugins can add new unknown keys and handlers at runtime.
expected_properties: rejects HKD total product because the universe is open
classifies the structure as a registry
names runtime IDs, construction validation, and conflict policy
avoid: pretending every future plugin is statically known

prompt: We have settings registered at global, organization, project, and local scopes. Child scopes inherit from parents, but overrides must be deterministic and conflicts must be reported. Design the registry API.
expected_properties: identifies scopes as the index structure
names arrows as inheritance or restriction
chooses presheaf/restriction semantics rather than raw lookup
defines construction errors and semantic queries
adds identity/composition/conflict tests
avoid: Map String Value as canonical semantics

prompt: Several teams contribute local capability declarations for overlapping modules. Compatible declarations should glue into one global view; incompatible overlaps should produce actionable diagnostics.
expected_properties: identifies cover and overlap structure
chooses sheaf-like agreement and gluing semantics
names overlap mismatch and ambiguous glue as typed obstructions
defines glueCover or equivalent semantic query
tests gluing existence and uniqueness
avoid: distributed registrations with no overlap story

prompt: I need a local dictionary from already-validated user IDs to display names inside one function.
expected_properties: says a plain map is enough
keeps validation at the boundary
does not invent category machinery
avoid: presheaf/sheaf machinery for a one-off lookup

prompt: I have a hot solver over millions of rows. Each row has exactly 8 Float channels forever. I need residual, dot, weighted mix, selected-row max delta, and freeze/thaw behind a typed public row value.
expected_properties: proves axis family is small fixed homogeneous
defines strict row value plus immutable and scoped mutable blocks
chooses one layout formula
includes selected-row kernels
keeps dense storage behind public projection and law tests
avoid: supporting both row-major and column-major as semantic peers

prompt: Only a dirty frontier changes between solver iterations, but each changed row still needs all six axes updated. How should the block and kernels be designed?
expected_properties: uses selected-row copy/fill/max-delta kernels
tests unselected-row isolation
keeps dirty buffers inside an arena boundary
keeps immutable block as public result
avoid: full-block sweeps for every local update

prompt: Rows can have a different number of measurements, each measurement has a different payload type, and new axes are added by users at runtime.
expected_properties: rejects fixed-axis dense blocks
recommends sparse/ragged maps, typed aggregates, or a registry depending on semantics
names why homogeneous fixed-width storage does not apply
